snippets.title: Compare linear vs RBF SVM on XOR
snippets.code:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.svm import SVC

X = np.array([[0, 0], [1, 1], [0, 1], [1, 0]])
y = np.array([0, 0, 1, 1])  # XOR labels

linear = SVC(kernel="linear").fit(X, y)
rbf = SVC(kernel="rbf", gamma=2.0).fit(X, y)

print("linear train acc:", linear.score(X, y))
print("rbf train acc:", rbf.score(X, y))
snippets.explanation: Linear kernel cannot fit XOR; RBF kernel uses implicit φ to make the problem separable.

snippets.title: Polynomial kernel hyperparameters
snippets.code:
from sklearn.svm import SVC
from sklearn.datasets import make_moons
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split

X, y = make_moons(n_samples=300, noise=0.2, random_state=0)
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.3, random_state=0)

clf = SVC(kernel="poly", degree=3, gamma="scale", coef0=1)
clf.fit(X_train, y_train)
print("test accuracy:", clf.score(X_test, y_test))
snippets.explanation: degree=d sets interaction order; gamma scales dot products; coef0=r is the polynomial offset.

snippets.title: Build a Gram matrix manually
snippets.code:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.metrics.pairwise import rbf_kernel

X = np.array([[1.0, 2.0], [2.0, 3.0], [0.5, 1.5]])
K = rbf_kernel(X, gamma=0.5)  # K[i,j] = exp(-gamma * ||x_i - x_j||^2)

print("Gram matrix shape:", K.shape)
print("symmetric:", np.allclose(K, K.T))
print("diagonal all ones:", np.allclose(np.diag(K), 1.0))
snippets.explanation: SVM dual solvers consume the Gram matrix of pairwise K(x_i, x_j); RBF diagonal entries are 1 when comparing a point to itself.

snippets.title: Grid-search RBF gamma and C
snippets.code:
from sklearn.svm import SVC
from sklearn.model_selection import GridSearchCV
from sklearn.datasets import load_breast_cancer
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split

X, y = load_breast_cancer(return_X_y=True)
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.25, random_state=0)

search = GridSearchCV(
    SVC(kernel="rbf"),
    param_grid={"C": [0.1, 1, 10], "gamma": ["scale", 0.01, 0.1, 1]},
    cv=3,
)
search.fit(X_train, y_train)
print("best params:", search.best_params_)
print("test accuracy:", search.score(X_test, y_test))
snippets.explanation: Kernel methods need tuning: C controls margin slack; gamma controls RBF locality — both affect bias-variance.
